body_txt: Swashbuckling, rum-swilling, Dressed to Plunder pirates in modern times. This trope covers works where the presence of pirates clearly based on The Golden Age of Piracy , whether the real deal or the Hollywood versions, in works clearly set in the modern era or, at least, any point after the heyday of Caribbean piracy ended. Exactly why these characters are dressed and acting like they are varies from case to case. Some will be consciously trying to reenact "classical" piracy for one reason or another, and deliberately take up the dress, gimmicks, and mannerisms of pop culture piracy. Other cases will consist of holdouts of "traditional" pirates determinedly refusing to let go of the good ole ways. Others still will simply have peg-legged, eyepatch-wearing, doubloon-seeking Blackbeard stand-ins roam the seas of the 21st century without comment or explanation. Compare and contrast Sky Pirate . Space Pirates is when they are a few more centuries late; many cases of this trope are just as inexplicably tied to the visual icons of Caribbean pirates as these ones are. For modern, Real Life pirates of the type who are very good at shivering people's timbers (with an AK-47, not a cutlass), see Ruthless Modern Pirates , although overlap with this trope is possible. If the pirates are more concerned with looking the part than acting it, they're probably The Pirates Who Don't Do Anything , or maybe a Friendly Pirate . Compare to Born in the Wrong Century for when they feel like they belong in The Golden Age of Piracy . If a child expresses their wish to be a pirate when they grow up, it overlaps with Absurd Career Goals .
